expected_behavior: Dashboard data generation includes a parsed Benchmark Reports view model from a real live_codex_exec benchmark report while preserving no-lift and negative-delta state.
failure_mode: Dashboard boot data omits benchmark report state, uses mocks, hides negative delta, or claims productivity lift.

expected_behavior: The Benchmark Reports UI renders no-lift benchmark evidence with source refs, delta, next action, repair target, failure mode, and no command names.
failure_mode: Dashboard shows benchmark metrics without review evidence or exposes run/repair/write commands.

expected_behavior: Missing benchmark reports render explicit empty state instead of invented green status.
failure_mode: Dashboard invents benchmark evidence or hides the explicit missing-report state.

expected_behavior: Invalid benchmark reports render blocked state with invalid report path and parse summary.
failure_mode: Dashboard hides invalid benchmark reports or presents them as ready evidence.

expected_behavior: Negative-delta live benchmark evidence keeps productivity_lift_claimed false and routes to larger-suite or repair work before any product claim.
failure_mode: A one-task negative-delta report is overclaimed as productivity improvement or converted into a dashboard command.
